The cost of hiring a single candidate is rarely calculated. If organizations study this process deeply they will realize that the costs are fairly steep.

job boards

step\_02 
Review your applicants

Time spent

Preparing short list of candidates to contactAssuming just 250 responses (you may get more in most of the cases) 
Recent report suggests employers spends 6 seconds to 5 minutes (generous employer) reviewing each resume

2.5 hours 
to 
23.5 hours 

$62.50 
to 
$587.50

External cost

Cost of ATS (ranges as per ATS you are using) 
Large companies spend 7% of their recruiting budget on ATS. 
Also, ATS system requires human review as it filters candidates on words and phrases in the resume. 

\-

The human resource of every organization accomplishes many tasks with pre-assigned budgets. Hiring the right candidates is one of the most important tasks that they need to do using cost-effective techniques and tactics.  So, to make a cost-effective hiring decision & avoid the risk of the bad hires, iMocha’s candidate assessment tool is the key to improve your hiring metrics.

## Top 11 Talent Management Assessment Tools in 2026

Talent management assessment tools help organizations evaluate employee skills, competencies, potential, and performance to make informed decisions about hiring, development, succession planning, internal mobility, and workforce planning. These tools provide data-driven insights that enable HR and business leaders to identify skill gaps, align talent strategies with organizational goals, and build a future-ready workforce.

* Aaditya Mandloi
 
* 38 min read
 

## Top 10 Skills Taxonomy Software in 2026

Skills taxonomy software helps organizations create and manage a structured framework for defining, organizing, and tracking workforce skills. By standardizing skill data across roles, teams, and business functions, these platforms enable better talent decisions, from skills-based hiring and workforce planning to internal mobility and employee development.

* Aaditya Mandloi
 
* 41 min read
 

## Top 10 Competency Assessment Tools in 2026

Competency assessment tools evaluate employees' and candidates' skills, knowledge, behaviors, and job-specific competencies against predefined role requirements. Organizations use these tools to improve hiring accuracy, identify skill gaps, support workforce planning, and guide employee development initiatives.

* Kartik Mishra
 
* 45 min read
